title: [ImportVerilog] Add HierarchicalNames.cpp to support hierarchical names.

features.name: downward hierarchical name reference from parent to child instance
features.description: Code should include a downward hierarchical name reference where a parent module reads a variable from an instantiated child module using the dot-separated path syntax (instance.variable)

features.name: upward hierarchical name reference from child to parent module
features.description: Code should include an upward hierarchical name reference where a child module reads a variable from its parent module using the absolute hierarchical path (TopModule.variable)

features.name: sibling hierarchical name reference between unrelated modules
features.description: Code should include hierarchical name references between two sibling modules at the same hierarchy level that mutually reference each other's variables via absolute module paths

features.name: hierarchical name reference into multiply-instantiated module
features.description: Code should include hierarchical name references targeting variables inside a module type that is instantiated more than once, accessing different instance paths of the same module definition
